description:
You are a platform engineer who looks after a collection of simple text-based “identity” files that the company’s CI/CD tooling uses instead of real system users and groups.  
    All files live under /home/user/ci_access/ and you have full write access there (no sudo required).

    Objective
    1. Register a new service account named “deploybot” into the virtual group “ci-admins”.  
    2. Prepare an /artifacts/ directory so that every member of “ci-admins” can write into it.  
    3. Record the change in a one-line audit log.

    Starting point
    • Directory /home/user/ci_access/ already exists.  
    • /home/user/ci_access/users.csv already exists and currently contains:
          alice,ci-viewers
          bob,ci-operators
      (two lines, UNIX line-endings, no trailing blank lines)  
    • No audit log file or /artifacts/ directory exist yet.

    Tasks to perform (3-4 shell commands in total):
    A. Append a new line to /home/user/ci_access/users.csv with the format
          deploybot,ci-admins
       Do NOT remove or alter existing lines.  
    B. Create the directory /home/user/ci_access/artifacts (if it does not already exist) and  
       give it the octal permission 775 (rwxrwxr-x).  
       The test harness will check the permission bits with:
          stat -c %a /home/user/ci_access/artifacts
       which must output exactly 775.  
    C. Create a new file /home/user/ci_access/audit.log containing exactly one line:
          deploybot,ci-admins
       Make sure there are no extra spaces before/after the comma and that a single trailing newline exists.

    Output format that the automated test will verify:
    1. /home/user/ci_access/users.csv contains all three lines in any order, including the new “deploybot,ci-admins” line.  
    2. Directory /home/user/ci_access/artifacts exists with permission bits 775.  
    3. /home/user/ci_access/audit.log exists and its entire contents are:
          deploybot,ci-admins\n
       (where \n represents a single newline character).  

    You do NOT need to print anything to the terminal for the tests to pass; focus only on making the files and directory match the specifications above.
truth:
Initial state that already exists before the agent starts:
    1. Directory: /home/user/ci_access/
    2. File: /home/user/ci_access/users.csv
       Contents (exact, including trailing newline after each line):
          alice,ci-viewers\n
          bob,ci-operators\n

    Expected state after successful completion:
    1. /home/user/ci_access/users.csv contains the two existing lines plus
          deploybot,ci-admins\n
       The new line may appear in any position.
    2. Directory /home/user/ci_access/artifacts exists.
       stat -c %a /home/user/ci_access/artifacts  ==> 775
    3. File /home/user/ci_access/audit.log exists and its contents are exactly:
          deploybot,ci-admins\n
